title: Meeting Your English Teacher
learningGoal: The learner will successfully articulate 4 spoken introductory SVO sentences in English, maintaining proper time-based greetings and polite responses in a real-time roleplay.
instructions: In this guided roleplay, you are entering your new English classroom in the morning. You will talk to your teacher. Read the scenario, listen to the teacher's prompts, and record your spoken English response. Try to match the sample audio's rhythm.

contentItems.text: Speak clearly. Pay attention to the 'W' in 'Welcome' and 'V' in 'Very' if you use them.

speakingPrompt: Respond verbally to the teacher's prompts to introduce yourself.
scenario: It is 9:00 AM. You open the door to the classroom. Your new English teacher, Mr. Smith, is standing at the desk.

subTasks.prompt: शिक्षक आपकी ओर देखते हैं। उन्हें 'शुभ प्रभात' (सुबह 9 बजे) कहें और सर कहें।
शिक्षक कहते हैं, 'Good morning! Welcome to the class. What is your name?' अपना नाम बताएं (My name is... का उपयोग करके)।
शिक्षक कहते हैं, 'Nice to meet you, Amit. How are you today?' उन्हें बताएं कि आप ठीक हैं और धन्यवाद दें।
शिक्षक कहते हैं, 'Please sit down. The class will start now.' शांति से उन्हें धन्यवाद दें।

scenarioContext.baseTranslation: आपकी अंग्रेजी कक्षा में स्वागत है।

scoringRubric.Pronunciation: Checks for clear V/W distinction and understandable phrasing.
scoringRubric.Completeness: Did the user fully answer the WH-questions?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Good morning, sir. My name is Amit. I am fine, thank you. Thank you, sir.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You have successfully held your first English conversation. Your teacher understood you perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Ensure you are saying 'My name is' and correctly replying 'I am fine, thank you'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good attempt! You missed a few politeness markers like 'Thank you' or your pronunciation of 'morning' was difficult to understand.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Teacher: Good morning! Welcome to the class. What is your name? ...

hintText: Don't rush. Speak slowly and pronounce each word fully: 'My... name... is... Amit.'
commonMistakes: Forgetting to say Thank you when asked 'How are you?'
